data.title: Vance Reiterates Claim That Iran’s Naval And Military Power Has Been Destroyed
data.description:
U.S. Vice President Vance reiterated that Iran’s naval and military power has been destroyed.

According to Jin10, Vance repeated the claim without providing additional details or evidence.

data.title: U.S. Vice President Vance Says U.S. Carried Out Airstrikes After Iran Fired on Ships
data.description: U.S. Vice President Vance said the United States carried out airstrikes and dropped bombs after Iran fired on ships a few days earlier. According to Jin10, Vance said the U.S. action followed the incident in which Iran opened fire on vessels.

data.title: U.S. Four-Month Treasury Bill Auction Bid-To-Cover Rises To 2.66
data.description:
The U.S. four-month Treasury bill auction recorded a bid-to-cover ratio of 2.66 as of July 1.

According to Jin10, the previous bid-to-cover ratio was 2.55.

data.title: US Won’t Renew USMCA, Shifts to Annual Reviews, Trade Representative Greer Says
data.description:
The US decided against renewing its trade deal with Canada and Mexico and will instead conduct annual reviews of the USMCA, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said, according to Bloomberg.

The move risks adding uncertainty for companies producing goods across North America.

data.title: STOCKS | Stocks Rebound on Manufacturing Data, Warsh Says Inflation Risks Have Eased
data.description:
Stocks rebounded from session lows after signs of economic strength and easing price pressures, with Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh saying inflation risks have come down, according to Bloomberg.

Jordan Jackson, a global market strategist at JPMorgan Asset Management, discussed his macro view and the market reaction he expects from Thursday’s jobs data.

data.title: STOCKS | India Asks Meta to Explain WhatsApp Username Feature Within Three Days
data.description:
India’s Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has asked Meta Platforms to submit a detailed explanation of WhatsApp’s “username” feature within three days. According to Jin10, the request was made in an official letter from the ministry to the company.

The report did not provide further details on the ministry’s specific concerns or what information it requested beyond a detailed description of the feature.

data.title: U.S. Envoy Says Some European Countries Are Falling Short on Defense Spending Pledges
data.description:
A U.S. envoy said some European countries are not doing enough to meet their defense spending commitments ahead of a summit with U.S. President Donald Trump in Ankara, Turkey.

According to Jin10, the U.S. ambassador to NATO warned that several European allies were lagging in fulfilling agreed defense spending goals.

The envoy, Matthew Whitaker, said NATO leaders agreed at a meeting in The Hague last year to raise defense-related spending to 5% of GDP by 2035, following pressure from Trump.

Whitaker said some allies were doing more than others, naming Poland, Nordic countries, Baltic countries, and Germany as leaders.

He added that some countries were behind, either because current spending was insufficient or because they lacked a practical and workable path to meet the defense commitments agreed in The Hague.

Whitaker said Trump fully expected all allies to act immediately, move onto a path toward the 5% target, and do so as quickly as possible.

data.title: Meta Adds Verified Name Protections to WhatsApp Username Feature, Company Says
data.description: Meta Platforms Inc. said Wednesday it will allow high-profile names to be claimed only by legitimate owners as part of its effort to counter impersonation on WhatsApp’s new username feature, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Toyota’s U.S. EV Sales Rise 35% Year on Year in June
data.description: Toyota Motor’s electric vehicle sales in the United States totaled 122,063 units in June, up 35% from a year earlier. According to Jin10, the figure reflects year-on-year growth in Toyota’s U.S. EV sales for the month.

data.title: Google Cloud Confirms More Secure Option for EU Public Sector Organizations
data.description: Google Cloud confirmed it will offer a more secure option for European Union public sector organizations after its data protection impact assessment in the Netherlands received approval. According to Jin10, the company said the approval supports providing EU public sector bodies with a safer choice for cloud services.

data.title: Forward Industries Shares Rise Nearly 17% After Adding 500,000+ SOL in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
Forward Industries acquired more than $38 million worth of Solana during fiscal Q3, adding more than 500,000 SOL to its holdings. According to NS3.AI, the firm now holds around 7.55 million SOL.

Following the disclosure, Forward Industries’ shares jumped nearly 17% to $4.94 on Wednesday.

data.title: MSFTB Reaching a New All-Time High, Increase of 4.71% in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 01, 2026, 15:17 PM(UTC). according to Binance Market Data, MSFTB has achieved a new all-time high, trading at 388.07 USDT. The 24-hour increase of 4.71%

data.title: Warsh Taps Ex-BOE Chief King to Co-Chair New Fed Communications Task Force
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has tapped former Bank of England Governor Mervyn King to co-chair the US central bank’s new task force on communications, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: S&P 500 Reverses Earlier Losses and Turns Higher
data.description: The S&P 500 reversed earlier declines and moved into positive territory. According to Jin10, the index turned higher after previously trading lower.

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court Ruling on FTC Removal Authority Adds Pressure on SEC and CFTC Crypto Oversight
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court ruled on June 29 that U.S. President Donald Trump had the authority to remove Federal Trade Commission Commissioner Rebecca Slaughter, overturning Humphrey’s Executor, a 1935 precedent on removal protections for independent agencies.

According to NS3.AI, the decision could increase pressure on the Securities and Exchange Commission and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission as the agencies pursue coordinated oversight of the cryptocurrency sector.

The report also noted that the CLARITY Act has heightened the stakes, after the Senate Banking Committee advanced the bill by a 15-9 vote in May.

data.title: STOCKS | Micron Shares Swing After Class-Action Complaint and Cantor Fitzgerald Raises Target
data.description:
Micron shares fell as much as 9.5% after a class-action complaint named Micron, Samsung Electronics, and SK Hynix, before the stock closed about 1% higher.

According to NS3.AI, Cantor Fitzgerald raised its Micron price target from $1,500 to $2,000 and described Micron as a top pick in the semiconductor space.

data.title: Sugar Hits Highest Since Mid-May as El Niño Risks Spur Short Covering
data.description: Raw sugar futures climbed to the highest level in more than a month as concerns about El Niño prompted traders to cover short positions, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: A-Share Solar Inverter Stocks Slide July 1; Sungrow Falls Over 13%
data.description:
China’s A-share solar inverter sector weakened on July 1, with Sungrow Power Supply (300274.SZ) dropping nearly 20% intraday and ending down more than 13%, taking its market capitalization below 300 billion yuan, according to Jiemian News. Ginlong Technologies (300763.SZ) closed down 8.67%, while GoodWe (688390.SH), Sineng Electric (300827.SZ), Deye (605117.SH) and Airo Energy (688717.SH) also fell.

Several listed companies told Jiemian News they had heard market rumors about potential overseas market access restrictions for inverter products, but said no policy has been formally implemented and key details such as scope, timeline and enforcement remain unclear. Sungrow said its inverters exported to the US and other markets do not have remote design or wireless communication functions and meet relevant standards. Ginlong said its US business is under 100 million yuan and expects limited direct impact; Deye said the US accounts for about 2%-3% of its sales.

Citing Great Wall Securities data, the report said China’s inverter exports totaled about $943 million in May 2026, up 13.66% year-on-year and 0.63% month-on-month, with export volume at about 4.5719 million units.

data.title: Nissan CEO Espinosa Says 25% US Tariff Hurts Sales of Mexico-Made Cars
data.description:
Nissan Motor Co. President and CEO Ivan Espinosa said US tariffs are making it harder to sell Mexican-made cars in the US, with those vehicles facing a 25% rate, according to Bloomberg.

Espinosa also discussed the future of autonomous driving and Nissan’s investment plans in the US in an interview on “Bloomberg Surveillance.”

data.title: SEC Commissioner Peirce Expects Clarity Act to Pass This Summer After House Approval
data.description:
SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce said she expects the Clarity Act to pass this summer after the bill cleared the House and awaits action in the Senate. According to NS3.AI, the legislation would split oversight of cryptocurrencies between the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC).

Peirce said the bill would establish a federal framework for spot crypto markets.

SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the agency should bring digital asset innovators back under American law.
